title: Quiz: Time Value of Money
description: Test your understanding of present value, future value, and discounting concepts

questions.question: If you invest $1,000 at 8% annual interest for 10 years with annual compounding, what is the approximate future value?
What is the present value of $5,000 to be received in 3 years, discounted at 6%?
Which compounding frequency produces the highest future value for the same nominal interest rate?
questions.explanation: Continuous compounding gives the maximum possible growth, as interest compounds infinitely many times per period.

questions.question: What is the present value of a perpetuity paying $100 per year with a 5% discount rate?
A bond trading at a premium has a yield to maturity higher than its coupon rate.
questions.explanation: False. A bond trades at a premium when yield < coupon rate. Investors pay more because the coupon payments are more attractive than current market yields.

questions.question: An investment has an NPV of -$5,000. This means:

options.text: The investment destroys $5,000 of value at the given discount rate
The investment returns 5% less than required

questions.explanation: A negative NPV means the present value of cash inflows is less than the investment cost by $5,000, destroying shareholder value.

questions.question: The formula for continuous compounding future value is:
questions.explanation: Continuous compounding uses the natural exponential function: FV = PV × e^(rt)

questions.question: Which factors cause money to have time value? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Opportunity cost, inflation, and risk/uncertainty are the three main reasons money has time value. Tax policies affect returns but aren't a fundamental cause of time value.
